comment.body:
I tell you a "secret" how can you spare hundreds if not thousands of dollars a year...
If you buy Graham - Intelligent Investor, 
Natenberg - Options Volatility and Pricing, 
Bennett - Trading Volatility and 
Taleb - Dynamic Hedging (very advanced), 
you spent 185-230 USD only once, (but note that these books are avaible online for free in PDF format)
If you are an IBKR user, real time options + futures data for equities is approximately around $600/year
Lets say you spend $97/month for SG pro, or $224/month for OD promax (including my -10% discount)
It is $3543 in a year, assuming you bought the books and didn't used the discount.

A lot of furu makes money of the laziness of ppl, and literally just interpreting other ppl's thoughts as their own, and ask $100+ for a month, literally just to tell you the content of these books, and mostly with mistakes.
Also selling you useless and inaccurate metrics, naive option and vol models, and cannot properly read the outputs...

comment.body:
This is also crucial for the Fed.
It plans to hold its policy rate steady—even though recent inflation readings have been benign—bcs policymakers remain highly attuned to inflation expectations and tariff‐related uncertainties that could reignite price pressures. Geo + tariffs.
Fed desires to anchor expectations rather than risk repeating past mistakes of premature easing, noting that businesses and workers may act on even small upticks in expected inflation by raising prices and wage demands
